scope_note: Stanford's official testing page gives the current first-year testing policy and class-wide middle-50% ranges for students in the Class of 2028 who submitted scores. Stanford does not publish per-major testing ranges, so this panel stays school-wide.
policy: ACT or SAT scores are required for first-year applicants. Stanford says there are no minimum test scores required for admission and no score that guarantees admission.

claims.claim: Standardized testing is one of the application requirements that can highlight academic preparedness.
Please self-report your highest ACT and/or SAT scores in the testing section of the application; Stanford reviews either self-reported or official scores for application review.
If admitted and enrolled, official ACT and/or SAT scores matching the self-reported results are required.
Stanford does not have a preferred exam (ACT vs. SAT).
